summary: Iteration 4 backend testing: 14/14 pytest tests PASSED. Verified (1) auth gate on /ads/{id}/download/image, /download/video, /publish — all return 401 without bearer; (2) tenant isolation — cross-tenant requests with another user's token return 404; (3) owner_id now correctly populated on Ad model and persisted to DB; (4) automatic logo watermarking via Pillow + Google Favicon — generated PNG bottom-right region shows the white badge (avg brightness 200 on latest variant); (5) publish_ad with owner token but no fb_access_token returns 400 with 'Set FB Access Token...' message; (6) variants are owner-gated, charge wallet, and also receive the watermark; (7) public /api/public/leads/{wid} still works without auth.

minor.issue: Error message still reads 'Set FB Access Token in Settings first' instead of 'in this website's settings first' — minor copy issue called out in iteration_3.json. Test still passes because the substring 'FB Access Token' matches. Consider updating wording for clarity since creds are now per-website.

Code still falls back to legacy global db.meta_settings collection (line ~1005). MetaSettings pydantic model is dead code per iteration_3 review. Not breaking, but should be removed for clean multi-tenant architecture.

Review request says POST /api/apply/{website_id} is the public lead endpoint, but actual backend route is POST /api/public/leads/{wid}. /apply/{id} is the FRONTEND path. No backend issue — clarification for main agent.

action_items: Optional: clean up legacy db.meta_settings fallback inside publish_ad (lines ~1005-1008 of server.py) and delete the now-unused MetaSettings pydantic class.
Optional: refine publish_ad error text to mention 'this website's settings' so users know where to set FB creds.
Optional: refactor /app/backend/server.py (1342 lines) into routers/services modules — flagged since iteration_3.
critical_code_review_comments: _apply_logo_watermark catches all exceptions silently (logger.exception only) — image is still saved without badge if Pillow fails. Acceptable for graceful degradation, but main agent may want to expose a 'watermark_failed' flag on the Ad doc so the UI can surface it.

_fetch_website_logo_bytes makes up to 3 outbound HTTP calls (HTML scrape, /favicon.ico, Google favicon API) with 6s timeouts each — worst case 18s added to ad generation latency on a slow site. Currently observed total ad-gen time ~25s per ad which is acceptable.

server.py is now 1342 lines (was 1162 in iter3) — well over 700-line threshold. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (ads, websites, leads, admin, auth) and services (llm, publish, watermark, scheduler).
Watermark code only writes PNG (line 419) but ad image is saved as .png so that's fine. Quality 235 alpha gives a tasteful semi-translucent white badge.
Variant endpoint correctly re-applies watermark via _generate_image_to_file — verified by test.
test_credentials: admin: manish99346626@gmail.com / Manish@1234 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Created ephemeral non-admin test_other_<uuid>@example.com / TestPass@123 for cross-tenant tests, auto-deleted via fixture teardown.
seed_data_creation: Fixture-scoped TEST_WatermarkSite (https://www.python.org) + 1 ad + 1 variant + 1 public lead — all auto-cleaned by fixture teardown. No persistent test data left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All security gates (auth + tenant isolation) on download_image/download_video/publish_ad are now correctly enforced. Logo watermark works for python.org (uses Google Favicon API since most ad-gen sites won't expose <link rel=icon>). Visual verification: bottom-right ~80x80 px region of generated PNG should have avg brightness >150 when watermark renders successfully. Tests skip Sora-2 video gen (cost). publish_ad's success path with REAL FB creds was not exercised — only the 'no token' 400 path. Backend file is now 1342 lines — refactor still pending.
rca of the issue: No bugs reproduced this iteration — main agent's iter-4 fixes (auth Depends on 3 endpoints + owner_id on Ad pydantic + Pillow watermarking) all work as designed. Only nit: publish_ad still references legacy global meta_settings as fallback, which is harmless but should be removed for clean multi-tenant code.
